layman_headline: The market is fearful and no coin is screaming 'buy me' — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin slipping below $63K amid geopolitical tensions and a leverage flush. All the coins on our watchlist have significant problems: some have already surged 30-60% and are likely overextended, while others have bearish chart patterns with sellers firmly in control. High Bitcoin dominance at 56% means money is flowing out of altcoins and into Bitcoin, making alt trades riskier than usual.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines. Don't chase coins that have already pumped (DCR +37%, DEXE +62%, VELVET +18%). Let the fear settle and watch for a cleaner setup where technicals, news, and chart structure all align. Capital preservation is key right now.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate fails the composite test. DCR's RSI of 19.6 is extremely oversold (high-confidence signal per our track record), but this is contradicted by a +36.6% 7-day move — the RSI hasn't caught up to the spike, making the oversold reading misleading. The chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs. DEXE is up 61.7% in a week with no chart data — pure momentum chase. JTO has the best chart bias (+0.40) but RSI at 59 is overbought-leaning and risk/reward is only 1.26x. ETHFI has a +0.60 chart bias with bullish marubozu, but resistance is only 2.5% away with terrible R:R of 0.11x. BTC dominance at 56.1% is a headwind for all alts. Fear & Greed at 28 is improving (from 24.6 weekly avg), which per our track record is a reliable signal — but it supports waiting for the turn to complete rather than front-running it.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.1% creates persistent headwinds for all altcoin positions
Geopolitical risks (Hormuz oil shock) could trigger another broad risk-off selloff taking BTC below $60K
All swing candidates show either overextended rallies or bearish chart structures — no clean setups exist
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 support — a break below would invalidate all alt setups
Fear & Greed index crossing above 35 — would confirm improving sentiment trend and give stronger entry signals
DCR price action near $11 support — if it pulls back and holds there, the oversold RSI bounce thesis becomes valid
market_context: Bitcoin is under pressure near $63K after an Asian-session leverage flush, with geopolitical risks from the Hormuz oil situation providing a bearish overhang. The stablecoin market has shrunk $10B since May, but BTC ETFs just snapped an 8-week outflow streak with $197M inflows — mixed signals that warrant patience.
context_banner: Fear is improving but hasn't turned — with all altcoin candidates either overextended or technically broken, disciplined patience beats forced entries.

key_signals.description: Fear at 28 improving from weekly avg of 24.6 — trend is positive but hasn't reached the inflection point that historically precedes strong alt rallies.
DCR RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold (our most reliable technical signal at 75% win rate), but the 37% weekly pump already occurred — the oversold reading is lagging, not predictive.
Bitcoin leverage flush below $63K and Hormuz oil shock headlines create risk-off environment unfavorable for new alt positions.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M snapping 8-week outflows — bullish for BTC but flows aren't reaching alts yet with dominance at 56.1%.
Best chart bias among candidates is ETHFI at +0.60, but its risk/reward ratio of 0.11x makes it untradeable — resistance is just 2.5% away.
